pr.title: refactor(frontend): canvas renderer and game shell rewrite
pr.body: ## Summary Rewrites the main game frontend around a dedicated **Canvas 2D renderer** instead of the previous mix of DOM board components and renderer logic embedded in React. ## What changed ### Architecture - **`BoardRenderer`** (`frontend/src/renderer/`) — owns all canvas drawing: covered/revealed cells, grid lines, chunk borders, mines/flags (vector shapes, not emoji) - **`useChunkStore`** — socket chunk subscribe/unsubscribe + delta merging (extracted from `ChunkLoader`) - **`GameView` / `GameCanvas` / `GameHud`** — thin React shell: HUD overlay, input handling, rAF paint loop - **`chunkWire.ts`** — wire-format → `Chunk` conversion shared by the store ### UX improvements - **Smooth pixel panning** (fractional viewport center) instead of snapping one cell per drag step - **HUD** with connection status, center/zoom/cell coords, lockout banner - Board stays visible while chunks load (banner instead of full-screen blocker) - **Stable chunk subscription keys** — visible chunks subscribe immediately; buffer region debounced; single subscription effect (fixes duplicate subscribe pattern) ### Removed - Legacy `Board`, `Cell`, `BoardSVG`, `ChunkLoader`, `CanvasBoard`, debug `SingleChunkPage` route - Old CSS / `.bak` JS files ## How to test ```bash docker-compose up --build # open http://localhost:8080 ``` - Pan (drag), zoom (wheel), WASD/arrow keys - Click reveal, right-click flag, double-click chord - Pan across chunk boundaries and confirm tiles render without duplicate subscription spam in network logs ## Notes - `CHUNK_SIZE` (32) and socket protocol unchanged — backend compatibility preserved. - `style.css` is no longer imported; styling lives in `frontend/src/styles/game.css`.
pr.author: mvb0005
